message:
[high] fcp-core: reject verify() resource_allow bypass when caller omits resource_uris

Cross-connector defense at the verifier. CapabilityVerifier::verify has
a resource_uris slice parameter that feeds enforce_resource_constraints.
The allow-list check at capability.rs:1654-1667 iterates over
resource_uris only when constraints.resource_allow is non-empty — but
an empty resource_uris slice means the for-loop runs zero times and
the allow-list silently passes. A host that issues
`resource_allow = ["notion://page/123"]` and hands the token to a
connector cannot rely on that scope being enforced because every one
of the ~76 `verifier.verify(req.capability_token, &cap, &op, &[])`
call sites in the connector tree passes &[] regardless of whether the
operation targets a specific resource.

Attack shape:
  1. Host mints token with resource_allow = ["notion://page/A"].
  2. Caller hands token to notion connector, asks it to delete page B.
  3. notion::connector.rs:1058 calls verifier.verify(.., &[]).
  4. enforce_resource_constraints runs with empty resource_uris; the
     allow-list loop iterates 0 times; constraint passes.
  5. Page B is deleted despite the token being scoped to page A only.

Fix: when resource_allow contains any non-wildcard pattern and
resource_uris is empty, refuse with FcpError::ResourceNotAllowed. A
pure wildcard (`["*"]`) is still treated as "unrestricted" and keeps
accepting empty resource_uris, so the ~20 connector integration-test
fixtures that use `resource_allow: vec!["*".into()]` remain green.
Connectors that currently pass &[] for operations with specific
resource URIs (notion/get_page, shopify/orders, etc.) are surfaced
as the real bugs they are — rejected loudly at verify time instead
of silently bypassing — rather than masked by a no-op loop.

- New non-wildcard detector in enforce_resource_constraints
  (capability.rs:1655-1678). Pure "*" remains exempt; mixed lists like
  `["*", "notion://page/A"]` take the non-wildcard branch and are
  checked strictly.
- New test helper test_constraints_cbor_with_resource_allow for
  building tokens with caller-specified patterns.
- Three new unit tests:
  * verify_rejects_non_wildcard_resource_allow_with_empty_uris —
    documents the attack shape and the new refusal.
  * verify_accepts_non_wildcard_resource_allow_with_matching_uri —
    confirms the happy path still flows.
  * verify_accepts_wildcard_resource_allow_with_empty_uris —
    backward-compat guard for the "*" fixtures.

Verification:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-p7 cargo test -p fcp-core --lib
  → 3937 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored (finished in 0.19s, full suite).
Zero regressions against existing verifier and constraint tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
